senses.definition: to travel or move to a foreign country or location, typically for the purpose of study, work, vacation, or residence

examples.x: She decided to go abroad for a semester of study in Europe.
They went abroad to explore new cultures and traditions.
Let's plan a trip to go abroad and experience different places.
Going abroad can offer opportunities for personal growth and new experiences.
The opportunity to go abroad for work was an exciting prospect.

senses.definition: to physically move or travel a significant distance, often involving long journeys, road trips, etc.

examples.x: They went far on their road trip, covering thousands of miles.
The marathon runners pushed themselves to go far in the race.
They decided to go far on their backpacking adventure, exploring remote regions.
The explorers went far into the wilderness to conduct their research.
Let's plan a road trip to go far and discover new places.

senses.definition: to take the initial action in a particular situation

examples.x: She decided to go first in the game of chess.
He volunteered to go first when presenting their project to the class.
Let's encourage teamwork by taking turns and deciding who should go first.
Going first in a race can provide an advantage by setting the pace.
She was always eager to go first in trying out new challenges.

senses.definition: to try to catch fish using a fishing rod and bait

examples.x: In a month, she is planning on going fishing with her friends.

senses.definition: to meet someone in a social or romantic context for the purpose of getting to know each other better

examples.x: He asked her to go on a date to the local cafe.
They went on a date to a fancy restaurant for their anniversary.
Let's set up a plan to go on a date and spend quality time together.
Going on a date can be an exciting way to build connections and explore relationships.
She agreed to go on a date with her coworker after he asked her out.

senses.definition: to have an outdoor meal or gathering in a natural or park setting

examples.x: They decided to go on a picnic in the nearby park for a family outing.
She invited her friends to go on a picnic by the lake.
Let's plan to go on a picnic and enjoy the beautiful weather.
She packed a basket with sandwiches and snacks to go on a picnic with her partner.
Going on a picnic is a delightful way to spend time outdoors with loved ones.

word_list.pronunciation: ɡˌoʊ ˈaʊt ɔːɹ ˌɑːn ɐ ɔːɹ stɹˈaɪk

senses.definition: (of a group of employees) to refuse to work as a form of protest or to demand changes to their working conditions, pay, or other employment-related issues

examples.x: The workers decided to go on strike to demand fair wages.
Going on a strike is a last resort when all other options for resolving disputes have failed.
They went on strike to protest against unsafe working conditions.
Let's hope the negotiations go well and the workers don't have to go on a strike.
She had to adjust her plans when public transportation workers went on strike.
The factory workers decided to go out on a strike to demand higher wages and improved safety measures.
If their demands for better working conditions are not met, the employees are prepared to go out on strike.

senses.definition: to travel way from home for fun or to explore new places

examples.x: They planned to go on a trip to Europe to explore historical sites.
She invited her friends to go on a trip to the mountains for a hiking adventure.
Let's save up for a vacation and go on a trip next summer.
Going on a trip can be a thrilling opportunity to discover new destinations.

senses.definition: to travel or move from one place to another by walking
to take actions or attack aggressively in a conflict or competition to gain an advantage

examples.x: The army decided to go on the offensive and launch an attack.
In the debate, he chose to go on the offensive by presenting strong arguments.
Let's be prepared to go on the offensive in negotiations to secure the deal.
Going on the offensive can be a strategy to gain the upper hand in a competitive market.
The team went on the offensive in the second half of the game, scoring several goals.

senses.definition: to access the Internet or be connected to the digital world through a computer, smartphone, or other electronic device

examples.x: She went online to check her email and social media accounts.
They decided to go online to research their upcoming vacation destination.
Let's go online and order the items we need from the e-commerce website.
The students often go online to access educational resources and assignments.
She went online to join the virtual conference with colleagues from around the world.

senses.definition: to travel to a foreign country

examples.x: They planned to go overseas for their honeymoon in a tropical paradise.
She wanted to go overseas to visit her relatives living in a different country.
Going overseas can be an enriching experience, offering exposure to different cultures.
The business expansion required them to go overseas to explore new markets.
She decided to go overseas to pursue higher education at an international university.

senses.definition: to travel on a sailboat or similar watercraft, using the wind to move

examples.x: They decided to go sailing on the serene lake during the weekend.
She went sailing as a way to escape the hustle and bustle of city life.
Going sailing involves handling sails and navigating on the water with wind propulsion.
She loves to go sailing in the open sea whenever she gets the chance.

senses.definition: to progress or proceed without problems or interruptions

examples.x: The project went smoothly, thanks to the effective collaboration among team members.
They hoped the transition to the new system would go smoothly.
Let's plan the event carefully to ensure that everything goes smoothly.
She was relieved to see that her travel plans were going smoothly without delays.

word_list.pronunciation: ɡˌoʊ tə bˈɛd ˌɑːn ɔːɹ ˈæftɚɹ ɐn ˈɑːɹɡjuːmənt

senses.definition: to sleep without resolving a disagreement or argument, resulting in lasting uneasy feelings

examples.x: Never go to bed after an argument as it makes it harder to be resolved the day after.
One shall not go to bed on an argument.

senses.definition: to walk in a way that is very fast and both feet are never on the ground at the same time, often for exercise or enjoyment

examples.x: After work, he went running to clear his mind and relieve stress.
We often go running together on weekends as a part of our fitness routine.

senses.definition: to enjoy moving to music at social events or gatherings

examples.x: On weekends, they love to go dancing at the local nightclub.
They went dancing to celebrate their friend's birthday.
The group of friends decided to go dancing downtown for a fun night out
Every Saturday night, they go dancing at the local club.
She loves to go dancing with her friends to unwind after a busy week.

senses.definition: to move on foot for exercise or enjoyment

examples.x: We like to go walking along the beach at sunset for relaxation.
Residents in the community often go walking together in the evenings for socializing and exercise.

senses.definition: to buy things from stores

examples.x: Let's go shopping for new clothes this weekend.
I have a list of things to buy, so I'm planning to go shopping tomorrow.
They decided to go shopping for furniture to decorate their new home.
She enjoys going shopping for fresh produce at the local farmers' market.
They often go shopping together to explore the latest fashion trends.

senses.definition: to be in the water and move for enjoyment or exercise

examples.x: During the hot summer days, we often go swimming at the community pool.
They decided to go swimming in the crystal-clear waters of the tropical beach.
After a long hike, the group went swimming in the refreshing mountain lake.
The kids were excited to go swimming in the backyard pool on a sunny afternoon.

senses.definition: to use one's mouth to stimulate another person's genital area as a form of sexual activity

examples.x: They engaged in intimate activities, with one of them going down on the other.
She decided to reciprocate the pleasure by going down on him.
They enjoyed exploring each other's bodies, including going down on one another.

senses.definition: to descend from the upper deck of a ship to the lower deck or interior areas of the ship

examples.x: As the storm approached, the captain ordered everyone to go below for safety.
The passengers on the cruise ship enjoyed the view from the top deck but had to go below when it started raining.
The crew members often had to go below to access the ship's control room.
When the storm hit, the passengers went below for safety.

senses.definition: to lie down in your bed to sleep, whether at night or for a nap during the day

examples.x: After I go to bed, I usually think about what happened during the day.
On a rainy day, it's tempting to go to bed and read a good book.
She always goes to bed at 10 PM to ensure she gets enough sleep.
She went to bed immediately after coming home from the party.
When you go to bed, don't forget to set your alarm for tomorrow.
